questions.title: Where does the reviewâ†’repairâ†’review loop live?
questions.context: Today `runMechanicalReviewExecutionLoop` (packages/review/src/index.ts:149) inlines `runExecutionDryRun` and re-runs review in a single function. Phase 4 just locked a real streaming executor with JSONL journal + lifecycle events. The Phase 5 loop has to drive both real execution and model review (plugged in Phase 8). Decision shapes who owns orchestration vs who owns verdict.

options.label: Keep it inside @protostar/review (extend the existing function)
options.description: Review package owns the loop; takes an injected `executor` and `modelReviewer`. Pro: minimal churn, contract already exists. Con: review imports execution-runtime concerns (signal, journal writer); package-boundary smell.

options.label: New top-level orchestrator package (`@protostar/loop` or similar)
options.description: A dedicated coordinator that consumes execution + review + (later) model-review as injected services. Pro: clean separation â€” review stays a verdict function, execution stays a runner. Con: a fifth package boundary to keep aligned with the others.

options.label: Drive it from `apps/factory-cli` (`runFactory`)
options.description: The CLI orchestrator stitches execution â†’ mechanical review â†’ (model review) â†’ repair â†’ re-execute. Pro: matches today's `runFactory` posture (already 1190 lines of orchestration). Con: pushes loop semantics into the app layer where it can't be unit-tested cleanly; admission boundary blurs.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Mechanical â†’ model serialization
questions.context: LOOP-01 says mechanical first, LOOP-02 says model second. Open question: is a mechanical-fail short-circuit (skip model entirely until mechanical passes) or do both always run for evidence purposes?

options.label: Strict serial â€” model only runs when mechanical passes
options.description: Mechanical fail â†’ repair-and-retry without ever invoking the model. Saves model budget; matches `mechanical-first` literal reading. Phase 8 panel costs nothing on broken builds.

options.label: Always run both, but mechanical is gating
options.description: Both verdicts captured every iteration; only mechanical-pass + model-pass â‡’ approved. Pro: judges accumulate critique evidence even on broken builds (useful for repair). Con: model runs against code that doesn't compile â€” mostly noise, real cost in Phase 8.

options.label: Conditional â€” mechanical-fail skips model, mechanical-pass-with-warnings runs model
options.description: Three-state mechanical (`pass | warn | fail`): `fail` short-circuits, `warn`/`pass` proceeds to model. More nuance but adds a state to keep coherent.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Re-execution scope on repair
questions.context: Phase 3 locked fresh-clone-per-run and tombstone-on-failure. Phase 4 Q-19 locked sequential per-task apply with run-level bail on apply-failure. When repair triggers re-execution, what runs again?

options.label: Re-run only the failed/repair tasks against the current workspace
options.description: Workspace persists across iterations within the same run; repair tasks re-execute on top of prior successful tasks. Cheapest. Tracks Phase 4's per-task apply model directly.

options.label: Fresh-clone-per-iteration: full plan re-executes top to bottom
options.description: Every repair iteration starts a new clone, replays all tasks. Maximum determinism, maximum cost. Matches the spirit of fresh-clone-per-run if you treat each iteration as a new run.

options.label: Re-run the failed task plus its dependents in the same workspace
options.description: Repair task + downstream subgraph; preserves earlier upstream successes. Middle ground. Requires plan-graph dependent computation in the loop.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: RepairPlan shape
questions.context: LOOP-03 requires a typed `RepairPlan` consumed by execution. Today review emits `repairTaskId` strings on findings (packages/review/src/index.ts:485). Phase 4 admission only knows about `AdmittedPlanExecutionArtifact`. What does the executor receive on a repair pass?

options.label: RepairPlan = list of `RepairTask` references with critiques attached
options.description: `{ runId, attempt, repairs: Array<{ planTaskId, critique: ReviewFinding[], modelCritiques?: JudgeCritique[] }> }`. Executor re-runs each named task, passing critiques into adapter context. Plan graph stays unchanged; we patch the *task input*, not the plan.

options.label: RepairPlan = a delta-shaped admitted plan
options.description: Repair returns a new `AdmittedPlanExecutionArtifact` with only the repair tasks present (and their dependsOn rewritten). Reuses the entire admission/handoff machinery; executor doesn't need a new code path. Cost: re-validating against admission rules every loop.

options.label: RepairPlan = critique bundle only; executor decides which tasks to retry
options.description: Review hands back critiques; executor's task scheduler picks which to re-run based on lifecycle event status. Minimal new types, but spreads the repair semantics across two packages.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: RepairPlan authorship
questions.context: Who synthesizes the RepairPlan from review findings + (Phase 8) model critiques? This is a load-bearing seam â€” Phase 8 plugs panel critiques in here.

options.label: Review package authors it (mechanical findings + model critiques flow in, RepairPlan flows out)
options.description: Single point of authorship; review owns the verdictâ†’repair translation. Phase 8 just supplies critiques to review; review structures them.

options.label: Separate `@protostar/repair` package
options.description: Pure transform: takes review verdict + critiques, returns RepairPlan. Phase 5 ships the type + a pass-through implementation; Phase 8/later adds policy (which critiques are repairable vs blocking).

options.label: Loop orchestrator authors it inline
options.description: Whoever owns the loop (Q-01) constructs the RepairPlan from raw findings. Cheapest; couples authorship to orchestration.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Critique propagation into adapter retry
questions.context: When a repair task re-runs, the LM Studio adapter must see *why* the previous attempt failed. Phase 4 Q-12 already has a parse-reformat retry. This is structurally different â€” it's a semantic critique from review.

options.label: Append critiques to adapter prompt as a structured `repairContext` field on the task
options.description: Task gets `repairContext?: { previousAttempt: AdapterAttemptRef, critiques: Critique[] }`. Adapter's prompt template reads it and adds 'Previous attempt failed: â€¦'. Clean, typed, testable.

options.label: Pass critiques through `ctx` (per-call) â€” never on the task itself
options.description: Loop injects critiques via `ctx.repairContext` when calling `adapter.execute`. Keeps the plan/task immutable across iterations. Slight downside: adapters that want to log this need separate plumbing.

options.label: Persist critique-as-evidence; adapter reads via `ctx.repoReader`-equivalent
options.description: Critiques written to `runs/{id}/review/iter-{N}/critique.json`; adapter receives only the URI and decides whether to read. Generic; possibly over-engineered for v0.1.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Build + lint check execution
questions.context: LOOP-01 lists 'build, lint, diff-touches-â‰¤1-file for cosmetic archetype, AC presence'. Build+lint require subprocess invocation against the workspace. Phase 3 ships subprocess capabilities behind authorized ops. Where does this actually run?

options.label: Mechanical review invokes subprocess directly via `@protostar/repo`
options.description: Review consumes `repoSubprocessRunner` (Phase 3) and runs configured commands (`pnpm verify`, `pnpm lint`) inside the workspace. Output captured as evidence. Pro: review owns the verdict end-to-end. Con: review now imports a runtime capability.

options.label: Execution stage runs build/lint as terminal tasks; review only inspects evidence
options.description: Plan includes implicit `build`/`lint` tasks at the tail. Execution emits their evidence. Review reads `evidence.json` and verdicts based on exit codes. Pro: review stays a pure inspector. Con: build/lint commands become plan concerns; the task graph balloons.

options.label: Hybrid: a `mechanical-checks` adapter that's invoked by the loop between execution and review
options.description: New adapter (sibling to lmstudio-coder) runs the configured checks. Review consumes its evidence. Pro: clean separation; reusable for non-cosmetic archetypes. Con: yet another adapter to design.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: `diff-touches-â‰¤1-file` cosmetic enforcement boundary
questions.context: Cosmetic archetype already exists in `packages/policy/src/archetypes.ts`. The 1-file constraint can be checked at multiple points: at admission (plan declaration), at execution (after each apply), or at review (post-apply diff inspection).

options.label: Review-only â€” inspect the run-level diff after all tasks apply
options.description: Review computes `git diff` between base and head of the run's branch and asserts â‰¤1 distinct file. Detects archetype escape regardless of how it happened. Definitive.

options.label: Execution-time per-task check + review-time run-level check (both)
options.description: Phase 4 already has per-task apply with hash-checked patches; cap each task's RepoChangeSet at 1 file *and* assert run-level total â‰¤1. Catches escapes at the smallest blast radius. More plumbing.

options.label: Admission-only â€” plan declares `expectedFiles` and admission rejects plans with >1
options.description: Catches the violation pre-execution. But model-generated diffs can violate even when the plan is conformant â€” admission alone isn't sufficient; need at least review.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: AC-presence check definition
questions.context: LOOP-01 says 'AC presence'. Where do acceptance criteria live, and what does 'presence' mean? Phase 1 admits intents; intents likely carry ACs. The mechanical check could mean (a) ACs declared, or (b) ACs covered by a test file in the diff.

options.label: Each AC must have a matching test ref recorded in the run evidence
options.description: Plan task records `acceptanceTestRef: { acId, testFile, testName }`; review verifies the test file exists in the diff and the test name appears. Tightest contract. Forces planner to pair ACs with tests.

options.label: ACs declared on intent + at least one assertion in the diff that mentions each ACid
options.description: Looser: any test file containing a string match against the AC id passes. Simple regex check. Cheap; tolerant of reasonable noise.

options.label: AC presence = ACs are declared and the build passes
options.description: Trust build + lint to indirectly cover AC verification (e.g., a failing test = missing AC coverage). Weakest. Likely insufficient â€” relies on the planner having generated the right tests.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Model-review interface in v0.1
questions.context: Phase 5 must lock the loop shape so Phase 8 plugs in semantic+consensus without reopening it. The seam decision is: how concrete vs abstract is the model-reviewer interface in this phase?

options.label: Typed interface only; v0.1 uses a stub that always returns `{ verdict: 'pass' }`
options.description: `ModelReviewer = (input: ModelReviewInput) => Promise<ModelReviewResult>`. Phase 5 ships the type + a `passthroughModelReviewer`. Phase 8 swaps in the real panel. Cleanest seam; v0.1 cosmetic-tweak runs are mechanical-only.

options.label: Minimal panel-of-one stub: one local LM Studio judge (Qwen3-80B) wired now
options.description: Phase 5 ships a real one-judge implementation; Phase 8 expands to N=2 panel + consensus. Pro: end-to-end real signal in v0.1. Con: introduces a second LM Studio adapter scope creep into Phase 5.

options.label: Stub that records hand-coded critiques from a fixture file
options.description: Useful for testing the loop's repair semantics deterministically. Phase 8 swaps for real panel. Pro: makes the loop testable without LM Studio. Slightly indirect.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Critique capture format
questions.context: Phase 8 will produce a panel of judge critiques. Phase 5 must lock the structural type now so the run-bundle schema doesn't shift later. LOOP-06 requires 'all judge critiques captured in the run bundle'.

options.label: Structured `JudgeCritique` with rubric scores + free-text rationale
options.description: `{ judgeId, model, rubric: Record<string, number>, verdict: 'pass'|'repair'|'block', rationale: string, taskRefs: string[] }`. Extensible; supports consensus math in Phase 8.

options.label: Free-text critique strings only, with severity tag
options.description: `{ judgeId, severity: 'minor'|'major'|'critical', text: string }`. Minimal. Fine for v0.1 mechanical; thin for Phase 8 consensus computation.

options.label: Discriminated union: one shape per critique kind (`build-failure`, `ac-uncovered`, `cosmetic-violation`, `semantic-issue`, ...)
options.description: Strongly typed per-kind. Best for downstream pattern matching. Highest churn cost when new critique kinds appear.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: `maxRepairLoops` source-of-truth
questions.context: LOOP-04 says shared budget, default N=3. Phase 4 already added `budget.adapterRetriesPerTask` and `budget.taskWallClockMs` to the capability envelope. Where does the loop budget live?

options.label: Capability envelope: new field `budget.maxRepairLoops` (default 3), signed in ConfirmedIntent
options.description: Mirrors Phase 4 Q-14/Q-15/Q-18 â€” single envelope bump cluster. Operator can change per-intent. Tamper-evident.

options.label: factory-config.json (file) with envelope override
options.description: Default in `factory-config.json` (Phase 4 Q-09); intent envelope can override per-run. Pro: easier ops tuning. Con: two sources of truth.

options.label: Hardcoded in @protostar/review with a function-arg override
options.description: Already partially done â€” `maxRepairLoops?: number` arg today (defaults to 0). Promote to default 3 and call it a day. Cheapest. No envelope plumbing.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Budget sharing semantics
questions.context: 'Shared budget' could mean (a) one counter for the whole loop, (b) separate counters for mechanical-only retries vs full mechanical+model loops, or (c) a hierarchical cap.

options.label: Single loop counter â€” every repair-and-retry costs 1, regardless of which reviewer flagged it
options.description: Simplest. N=3 means at most 3 repair iterations; doesn't matter if mechanical or model surfaced the issue. Matches LOOP-04 literal reading.

options.label: Two counters: mechanical-repairs + model-repairs (each capped separately)
options.description: Lets a stuck mechanical loop fail fast while letting model nuance get more retries (or vice versa). Operationally noisier; more knobs to tune.

options.label: Single counter, but adapter retries (Q-14 of Phase 4) nest under it
options.description: Total iterations Ă— per-task adapter retries gives the worst-case model-call count. Hierarchical envelope: `maxRepairLoops Ă— adapterRetriesPerTask`. Predictable upper bound on cost.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: `block` verdict evidence on exhaustion
questions.context: LOOP-06 requires 'evidence-bearing block verdict with all judge critiques captured'. What concretely lives in the block artifact?

options.label: Single `review-block.json` with full iteration history + all critiques + final diff URI
options.description: `{ runId, planId, status: 'block', reason, iterations: ReviewIteration[], finalDiffArtifact, exhaustedBudget: { maxRepairLoops, attempted } }`. One artifact, easy to ship in Phase 7 PR body.

options.label: Per-iteration JSONL (`review.jsonl`) + a small `review-result.json` summary
options.description: Mirrors Phase 4 journal+snapshot pattern. `review.jsonl` is the source of truth for individual critique events; `review-result.json` is the summary for fast paths (delivery refusal, status surface).

options.label: Append to existing execution journal â€” review events with `kind: 'review-*'`
options.description: Single journal, mixed event types. Fewer files; chronologically interleaved with execution events. Could complicate replay for resume.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: ReviewDecision artifact shape
questions.context: LOOP-05: 'only pass from both mechanical and model review allows progression to delivery'. Phase 7 needs to consume something that says 'both passed'. Decision shape determines the delivery contract.

options.label: Branded `DeliveryAuthorization` token returned by the loop on success
options.description: Type-level guarantee â€” Phase 7's `createGitHubPrDeliveryPlan` requires `DeliveryAuthorization` as input. Cannot be constructed except by a passing loop. Symmetric with Phase 1's `ConfirmedIntent` brand.

options.label: JSON artifact `review-decision.json` with `{ mechanical: 'pass', model: 'pass', authorizedAt }`; Phase 7 reads + validates
options.description: File-based gate. Easier to inspect post-hoc. Phase 7 validation must check both fields present + 'pass'. Token brand still possible by re-validating on read.

options.label: Both â€” artifact on disk for evidence + brand for type-level enforcement
options.description: Belt + suspenders. Mirrors Phase 1's `ConfirmedIntent` (file + brand). Most rigorous. Costs one extra read in delivery.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Delivery refusal layer
questions.context: Phase 7 (Octokit) is downstream. LOOP-05 says 'a delivery attempt with anything other than pass/pass is refused at the contract layer'. Where exactly does the refusal happen?

options.label: Type-level: delivery functions only accept `DeliveryAuthorization`
options.description: Compile error if you try to deliver without one. Pairs with Q-15a. Strongest static guarantee.

options.label: Authority-kernel admission gate: `authorizeDelivery(reviewDecision)`
options.description: Like Phase 2's other authorized ops â€” a gate that returns a branded `AuthorizedDeliveryOp`. Fits the Phase 2 pattern; uniform with subprocess/network ops.

options.label: Runtime check inside delivery package
options.description: `createGitHubPrDeliveryPlan` reads the file and throws if not pass/pass. Simplest. Loosest â€” any consumer skipping that function bypasses the gate.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Review iteration storage layout
questions.context: Phase 4 chose `runs/{id}/execution/journal.jsonl` + per-task subdirs. Review needs analogous structure. Question is granularity.

options.label: `runs/{id}/review/iter-{N}/{mechanical-result.json,model-result.json,repair-plan.json}`
options.description: Per-iteration directory. Easy to grep, easy to display in `inspect`. Slight directory bloat for runs with many iterations (capped at maxRepairLoops + 1).

options.label: Flat: `runs/{id}/review/{review.jsonl, review-result.json, repair-plans/{N}.json}`
options.description: Mirrors execution's flat layout exactly. Source of truth is JSONL; structured artifacts are sibling files. Smaller fs footprint; replay is simpler.

options.label: Hierarchical inside a single `review-bundle.json`
options.description: One file per run with all iterations nested. Smallest; but requires whole-file rewrite on each iteration â€” fragile under crash.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Review lifecycle events
questions.context: Phase 4 emits execution lifecycle events; Phase 9 will subscribe. Should Phase 5 emit a parallel review-event union (`review-iteration-started`, `mechanical-verdict`, `model-verdict`, `repair-plan-emitted`, `loop-approved`, `loop-blocked`)?

options.label: Yes â€” separate `ReviewLifecycleEvent` union, written to `review.jsonl`
options.description: Symmetric with execution. Phase 9 `inspect` and Phase 8 panel debugging benefit. Extra type to maintain.

options.label: Reuse execution lifecycle events, add `review-*` variants to the same union
options.description: Single event stream; richer chronology. Couples the two type unions â€” every review change touches execution's exported types.

options.label: Defer â€” only emit terminal events (`approved`/`blocked`/`repair-limit-reached`) for v0.1
options.description: Minimal lifecycle. Adequate for Phase 7 delivery gate. Phase 9 `inspect` will likely demand richer events later.

options.label: Custom
